title: Helping patients with gastroesophageal cancer avoid unnecessary chemotherapy
summary: A Peter Mac-led international clinical trial has found a way to personalize treatment for gastroesophageal cancer, identifying patients who could avoid further intensive rounds of chemotherapy while they recover from surgery. Each year more than 1,700 Australians are diagnosed with gastroesophageal cancer, which has a five-year survival rate of less than 25%.

title: New Study Highlights the Potential Impact of Funding Cuts on the HIV Response
summary: Up to 2.93 million more people could die from HIV-related causes due to international aid reductions.

title: A hidden strategy: How SARS-CoV-2 uses fibrinogen to evade the immune system
summary: As scientists, we often think we understand a virus—its structure, its tricks, the way it moves through the body. But every once in a while, we stumble upon something unexpected—something that completely changes the way we see an infection.

title: Women with chest pain may not need intensive medical therapy
summary: An intensive three-drug regimen of heart medications did not significantly impact the rate of serious cardiovascular events at five years in women who experienced signs and symptoms of ischemia, or insufficient blood flow to the heart muscle, but had no blockages in their heart's arteries, according to research presented at the American College of Cardiology's Annual Scientific Session (ACC.25).

title: Study highlights elevated suicide risks among musicians
summary: An Emory University study published in Frontiers in Public Health has identified significant suicide risks among musicians, shedding light on factors that contribute to the increased risk and highlighting the need for targeted mental health interventions within the music industry.

title: Exosomal RNA as a biomarker for prostate cancer detection
summary: Exosomal RNA is emerging as a promising biomarker for early prostate cancer detection.
